messages.content:
In which of the following is searching for a key value the fastest on average ?	
a. Height-Balanced Binary Search Trees
b. Wide Binary Search Trees
c. Binary Trees
d. Tall Binary Search Trees
a. Height-Balanced Binary Search Trees

true or false. In a min-heap, every key at level i+1 is greater than every key at level i, for every positive integer i<height.
 a. True
 b. False
a. True

true or false. For any AVL tree, its height is approximately half the number of nodes it has.
a. True
 b. False
b. False

Which of the following is the tightest bound for the worst-case of postorder depth-first traversal  on a binary tree with n nodes.
a. O(n)	
b. O(n lg n)	
c. O(lg n)	
d. O(n3)	
e. O(1)	
f. O(n2)
a. O(n)

Can represent how arithmetic, logical, and set expressions are to be evaluated bottom-up
a. Max-heap
b. k-ary Tree
c. AVL Tree
d. Syntax tree		
e. Min-heap		
f. Binary Search Tree
d. Syntax tree

Which will most likely require a rotation when a key k is added as a child of key p in an AVL tree ?		
a. k is the 2nd child of p and p has no sibling		
b. k is the only child of p and p has a sibling	
c. k is the only child of p and p has no sibling	
d. k is the 2nd child of p and p has a sibling
d. k is the 2nd child of p and p has a sibling

true or false. Priority queues dequeue elements with the best priorities first,  not by the order they were enqueued.
 a. True
b.  False
a. True

true or false. When creating syntax trees from postfix notation and an operator is encountered, the operator’s operands are popped from a stack.
a. True
 b. False
a. True

All children nodes have keys greater than their parents’ keys.	
a. Max-heap	
b. Syntax tree	
c. Min-heap	
d. Binary Search Tree	
e. k-ary Tree	
f. AVL Tree
a. Max-heap

true or false. In a binary search tree, the key values in the higher levels (i.e. lower level numbers) are lower than the key values in the lower levels.
 a. True
 b. False
b. False

Which of the following is the tightest bound for the worst-case of  delete(el) on a binary search tree with n nodes, including searching for el	
a. O(n3)	
b. O(1)	
c. O(n)	
d. O(lg n)	
e. O(n lg n)	
f. O(n2)
d. O(lg n)

Which of the following is the tightest bound for the worst-case of heapSort(n)  on an array with n values?
a. O(n3)
b. O(n2)	
c. O(n)	
d. O(n lg n)	
e. O(lg n)	
f. O(1)
d. O(n lg n)

Which data structure is most space efficient to represent k-ary trees for large k?	
a. Store the keys in an array according to their breadth-first traversal visit ordering	
b. Each parent points to its first child and its children form a linked list	
c. all of the other choices are as efficient as each other	
d. Nodes have a fixed number of children pointers equal to the maximum degree of the tree
b. Each parent points to its first child and its children form a linked list

true or false. The number of non-zero entries in the adjacency matrix representation of an undirected graph is even.
 a. True
 b. False
a. True

A collection of vertices and edges that connect pairs of vertices.	
a. Graph	
b. Tree	
c. Hash Table	
d. Linked List
a. Graph

true or false. In graphs, if all edges incident on a vertex are to be sequentially processed, an adjacency list representation often performs faster than an adjacency matrix representation.
a. True
b. False
a. True

Performed by Depth-First search when all adjacent vertices have all been visited	
a. Backtracking	
b. Divide-and-Conquer	
c. Collision Resolution	
d. Rotation
a. Backtracking

To compute shortest paths from a start vertex, what are the best adjacency matrix entries for pairs of non-adjacent vertices?	
a. 0
b. 1	
c. -∞	
d. ∞

true or false. Depth First Search of a graph uses a queue.
a. True
b. False
b. False

The number of edges in a graph with n vertices is	
a. O(lg n)
b. O(n)
c. O(n2)
d. O(1)
e. O(n lg n)

The number of edges can vary between graphs, but at most a complete graph of n vertices will have n*(n-1)/2 edges. Therefore, the tightest bound for the number of edges in a graph with n vertices is O(n2). So, the answer is c. O(n2).

true or false. Breadth First Search visits the vertices of an undirected graph one connected component after another.

 a. True

 b. False
a. True

Visits the vertices of a graph level-by-level
a. Depth-First Search
b. Breadth-First search
c. Dijkstra’s Algorithm
d. Greedy Algorithm
b. Breadth-First search

Draw the max-heap formed by inserting the following integer keys in this order :

30  20  10  40  8  15  25  35  13

then determine

 the root key (Enter one integer only or say null if none.  ex. 97 or null)

Draw the max-heap formed by inserting the following integer keys in this order :

30 20 10 40 8 15 25 35 13

then determine the left child of 25 (Enter one integer only or say null if none.  ex. 97 or null)

Draw the max-heap formed by inserting the following integer keys in this order :

30 20 10 40 8 15 25 35 13

then determine the right child of  35 (Enter one integer only or say null if none.  ex. 97 or null)

```
     40
   /     \
  30     25
 /  \    /  \
20  8  15   35
/\  
13 null null
```

Right child of 35: null

Draw the max-heap formed by inserting the following integer keys in this order :

30 20 10 40 8 15 25 35 13

then determine the height of this max-heap. (Remember: The root is at level 0.  Enter an integer.  ex. 97)
Height of the max-heap: 3
